game_name: Fire Emblem: Genealogy of the Holy War

danger_signals.visual_form: Movement range display on grid-based map
danger_signals.meaning: Visual indication of enemy attack reach and threat zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Castle capture state change on map
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of home base or strategic position indicates critical danger and mission failure condition

danger_signals.visual_form: Player army leader unit position relative to enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Leader defeat results in immediate game over and mission restart requirement

danger_signals.visual_form: Unit removal from battlefield after defeat
danger_signals.meaning: Fallen units except plot-critical ones are permanently lost from roster

danger_signals.visual_form: New enemy units appearing on map after castle seizure
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental state change (castle capture) triggers escalated enemy response

safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for weapon repair, item trading, and arena battles

safety_signals.visual_form: Save file capability at start of each turn
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency to preserve progress and mitigate permanent death risk
Family and married pairs provide critical hit bonuses and resource sharing safety
Stat boosts to nearby units indicate protective leadership presence

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Military units under Grannvalian command vs. corrupted/cult-aligned variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castles and territories controlled by Grannvale; player home base location

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Religious cult identity tied to revival of ancient dragon Loptous
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Cult-aligned enemy units distinguished from legitimate state forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Territories influenced by cult activity; scripted events showing cult dominance

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Founded by Twelve Crusaders; divine heraldry implied
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Nation-specific unit types and military configurations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Multi-region map structure reflecting eight distinct political entities

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Inherited heraldry from first generation; continuity of crusader legacy
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Stat values and skills visually influenced by parental attributes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle progression showing generational territory reclamation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle ownership display on map; accessible home base services
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Neighboring enemy castles deploy reinforcements; scripted objective changes trigger

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle visual state change indicating enemy ownership
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission failure if player's home castle seized; forced mission restart

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character roster swap; parental trait inheritance visible in stat displays
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Second-gen units inherit stat modifiers from parent assigned attributes; new relationship bonds form

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Married character pair proximity indicators on grid
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Critical hit boost activation; gold resource sharing; story-driven romantic scenes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy unit composition shift; cult-aligned forces become more prevalent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Story-driven enemy behavior changes; scripted cult-affiliated boss encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High-ranking unit visual prominence; aura radius indicator (three-tile range)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stat boost application to all units within leadership range; rank-dependent boost magnitude

environmental_storytelling.example: Seizing a castle triggers neighboring castle reinforcement deployment, showing escalating enemy response to player actions
Chapter structure spans two generations of characters across same continent (Jugdral), with territories shifting control between generations
Eight nations founded by Twelve Crusaders establish regional identity and territorial division; player progression reclaims crusader legacy territories
Enemy unit composition changes reflect growing Loptous cult influence across regions; story beats show cult stirring war between nations
Second generation units visually inherit stat traits from parents; appearance and skills communicate bloodline legacy and parentage

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Movement range displayed; critical to army survival; defeat triggers mission failure
Grant stat boosts to all units within three-tile radius; prioritized tactical targets
New units deploy from reinforcement points; dynamic troop escalation
Critical hit bonus activation; gold sharing capability; stat conversation boosts
Immediate permanent roster removal; irreversible loss for remainder of campaign
Visual/mechanical advantage/disadvantage based on rock-paper-scissors (lance > sword > axe > lance)

notes: Fire Emblem: Genealogy of the Holy War establishes world-state communication through grid-based tactical visualization and dynamic scripted events. Danger is communicated via permanent death mechanics, leadership dependency, and castle control stakes. Factions are distinguished through territorial control, unit composition, and generational inheritance systems. Political situation manifests through two-generation narrative structure showing crusader legacy conflict, cult infiltration, and inter-kingdom warfare. The game uses relationship mechanics (marriage, sibling bonds) as both narrative and mechanical danger-mitigation signals. World state changes visually through castle ownership, enemy reinforcement patterns, and character roster generational transitions.
